commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `rerank:false` to QMD v2 mcporter `query` calls when OpenClaw search mode is `search` or `vsearch`, plus a unit test for vector-only mode.

PR surface: Source +7, Tests +40. Total +47 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: configure QMD mcporter with `searchMode: "search"` or `"vsearch"` on current main and the unified `query` call omits `rerank`, while QMD defaults it to true.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed; the branch is already a focused implementation PR and I found no actionable patch defects.

target.title: fix(memory-core): don't run the LLM reranker in vsearch/search modes
target.body:
## What

`searchMode: "vsearch"` / `"search"` are documented as vector/lexical-only modes that must not do LLM work — `buildV2Searches`' own doc comment says they exist "so lexical-only or vector-only modes don't trigger unnecessary LLM/embedding work." But `runQmdSearchViaMcporter` builds `callArgs` without `rerank`, and QMD's unified `query` tool **defaults `rerank: true`**. So every `vsearch`/`search` memory search silently runs the LLM reranker anyway.

QMD's own tool description flags this: *"rerank … (default: true). Set to false for faster results on CPU-only machines."*

## Impact

- **Every deployment:** wasted LLM compute — the reranker model runs on searches that explicitly opted out of it.
- **CPU-only / GPU-constrained hosts:** the reranker stalls or hangs. On my Intel iGPU it triggers `vk::Queue::submit: ErrorDeviceLost`, which then poisons the embedding context and collapses warm recall into a CPU-fallback spin.

## Fix

In `runQmdSearchViaMcporter`, pass `rerank: false` for `search`/`vsearch` (full `query` mode keeps reranking). The cold/CLI path (`buildSearchArgs`) is unaffected — it already maps each mode to qmd's dedicated `vsearch`/`search`/`query` subcommands, which don't rerank. One line of behavior + a comment, plus a unit test asserting `vsearch` sends `rerank: false` (the existing `query`-mode test continues to assert it does **not**).

## Real behavior proof

Tested on a live OpenClaw gateway (memory-core, mcporter warm path) backed by QMD on an Intel UHD 630 (Gen9.5). Each case is a fresh `qmd mcp` process running exactly the `callArgs` openclaw builds; host kernel watched via `dmesg`:

```
BEFORE (current behavior — openclaw omits rerank):
  → openclaw sends: {"searches":[{"type":"vec","query":"how do I handle errors gracefully"}],"limit":3}
    Reranking error: [Error: vk::Queue::submit: ErrorDeviceLost]
    QMD Warning: GPU embedding error (ErrorDeviceLost) detected. Forcing CPU mode for this process.
  ← 12303ms  ERROR: vk::Queue::submit: ErrorDeviceLost
  >>> new host-kernel GPU HANGs during this call: 3

AFTER (this patch — rerank:false for vsearch):
  → openclaw sends: {"searches":[{"type":"vec","query":"how do I handle errors gracefully"}],"limit":3,"rerank":false}
  ← 3650ms  OK — 3 hits
  >>> new host-kernel GPU HANGs during this call: 0
```

Host kernel log during the BEFORE call:
```
i915 0000:00:02.0: [drm] GPU HANG: ecode 9:1:8ed1fff2, in libuv-worker [275718]
i915 0000:00:02.0: [drm] Resetting rcs0 for preemption time out
i915 0000:00:02.0: [drm] libuv-worker[277683] context reset due to GPU hang
i915 0000:00:02.0: [drm] GPU HANG: ecode 9:1:8ed1fff2, in libuv-worker [277683]
```

After applying this patch in my deployment, warm memory recall went from broken (CPU-spin) to ~126 ms on the GPU.

**Tests:** `pnpm test:extension memory-core` → the new test passes; all memory-core tests pass except `dreaming-phases.test.ts > suppresses cleanup warnings during request-scoped narrative fallback`, which is already red on clean `main` (verified by stashing this change) and unrelated to this PR.

---

🤖 **AI-assisted** — implemented with an AI coding agent; the real-behavior proof above is from my own live setup, not AI-generated. *Allow edits by maintainers* enabled. No `CHANGELOG.md` change (per CONTRIBUTING).
